# California: ongoing compliance after formation

## Secretary of State

File `SI-100`:

- initial filing within 90 days after registration;
- then every 2 years in the required filing window;
- also update when agent/address information changes.

Sources:

- https://www.sos.ca.gov/business-programs/business-entities/faqs
- https://www.sos.ca.gov/business-programs/business-entities/statements?86f7b=x

## Attorney General Registry of Charities and Fundraisers

After initial registration, California charities have annual AG renewal/reporting obligations.

Useful official pages:

- Initial registration: https://oag.ca.gov/charities/initial-reg
- Annual renewals/forms hub: https://oag.ca.gov/charities/forms

The AG materials incorporated by reference state:

- `Form RRF-1` is due no later than `4 months and 15 days` after the close of the accounting period;
- orgs with `$50,000` or more in total revenue generally also submit the applicable IRS `Form 990`;
- orgs with less than `$50,000` in total revenue generally file `CT-TR-1`.

Source:

- DOJ incorporated forms PDF: https://oag.ca.gov/system/files/media/pf-forms-incorporated-by-reference.pdf

## Franchise Tax Board

California-exempt organizations may have to file:

- `Form 199`;
- `FTB 199N`;
- `Form 109` if there is taxable unrelated business income.

Official sources:

- Annual and filing requirements: https://www.ftb.ca.gov/file/business/types/charities-nonprofits/annual-and-filing-requirements.html
- Form 199 booklet: https://www.ftb.ca.gov/forms/2022/2022-199-booklet.html

## IRS

Keep filing the correct annual federal return:

- `990-N`, `990-EZ`, or `990`;
- possible `990-T` if unrelated business income applies.

Official sources:

- Annual return due dates: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/return-due-dates-for-exempt-organizations-annual-return
- 990-N guidance: https://www.irs.gov/charities-non-profits/annual-electronic-filing-requirement-for-small-exempt-organizations-form-990-n-e-postcard

## Records you should maintain continuously

- articles and amendments;
- bylaws and amendments;
- IRS determination letter;
- FTB exemption acknowledgment;
- AG registration records;
- board minutes and written consents;
- conflict disclosures;
- grant agreements;
- donation acknowledgments;
- accounting records and annual budgets.

## Practical compliance notes for a research nonprofit

`gecon.ai` is likely low-risk if it:

- publishes research for public benefit;
- avoids private inurement and insider transactions;
- documents any founder compensation carefully;
- uses contracts when working with universities, contractors, or labs;
- separates charitable research from any commercial consulting or product work.
